We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Address Verification Agent
Job Locations: Ikorodu, Ketu, Ikeja, Mile 12 - Lagos, Ota, Abeokuta - Ogun, Ondo, Osun, and Oyo
Employment Type: Contract

Job Summary


	We are seeking field agents across the listed states/areas to conduct address verification for banks and fintechs. This is a part-time, contract-based role.


Requirements


	Willingness to work in the field.
	Possession of a functional smartphone.
	Ability to use Google Maps.
	Relevant qualifications are an added advantage.
	Prior work experience is not required.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@polarisdigitech.net using the Job Position and preferred location as the subject of the email.",

Customer Address Verification Agent Job at Polaris Digitech Limited




        
    

        
        Polaris Digitech Limited is a location intelligence firm that specializes in providing data, software and decision support services to fulfill its client’s strategies and management needs through the development and deployment of Geographic Information Systems (GIS).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Address Verification Agent
Job Locations: Ikorodu, Ketu, Ikeja, Mile 12 - Lagos, Ota, Abeokuta - Ogun, Ondo, Osun, and Oyo
Employment Type: Contract

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking field agents across the listed states/areas to conduct address verification for banks and fintechs. This is a part-time, contract-based role.


Requirements


	Willingness to work in the field.
	Possession of a functional smartphone.
	Ability to use Google Maps.
	Relevant qualifications are an added advantage.
	Prior work experience is not required.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and preferred location as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Manager (P-3)",
  "description": "United Nations International Children&#39;s Emergency Fund (UNICEF) works in some of the world&rsquo;s toughest places, to reach the world&rsquo;s most disadvantaged children. To save their lives. To defend their rights. To help them fulfill their potential. Across 190 countries and territories, we work for every child, everywhere, every day, to build a better world for everyone.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager (P-3)

Job no: 594084
Job Location: Abuja
Categories: Programme Management

How can you make a difference?


	As the Project Manager, you will lead overall project planning, scheduling, and execution to ensure the construction and operationalization of 3 vaccine hubs (Abuja and Kano), manage the operation and maintenance contracts for solarization of over 370 health centers, oversee the defect liability period for Abuja Premier Warehouse, operationalize the Lagos Hub and ensure they are all delivered on time, within scope, and within budget, in accordance with approved project objectives and work plans.
	You will report to the Nigeria Country Office Chief of Health and will work with the Health Managers responsible for leading the Primary Health Care, Immunization and maternal, neonatal and child heath (MNCH) clusters.


Major responsibilities


	Support to programme development and planning
	Programme management, monitoring and delivery of results&nbsp;
	Technical and operational support to programme implementation&nbsp;
	Networking and partnership building&nbsp;
	Innovation, knowledge management and capacity building&nbsp;


To qualify as an advocate for every child you will have
Minimum requirements:


	Education: An advanced University Degree (Master&rsquo;s or higher) in Health, Public Health Construction Management, Financial Management, Management, International Relations, or closely related field is required.
	Work Experience: A minimum of five years of relevant professional experience in the management of projects within the national/international health sector is required.


Skills:


	Ability to resolve difficult, complex and sensitive situations often under pressure;
	Ability to initiate and manage change in a diverse environment;
	Ability to clearly and concisely express ideas and concepts in written and oral form;
	Ability to allocate appropriate time and resources for successful achievement of goals, and foresee risks and allow for contingencies when planning;
	Ability to implement clear goals that are consistent with agreed strategies, identify priority activities and assignments, adjust priorities as required;
	Ability to work strategically to realize organizational goals, develop strategies, set clear visions;
	Ability to advanced communication and negotiation skills to establish and maintain trusted partnerships for achievement of objectives and to develop close and effective working relationships with diverse stakeholders;
	Ability to skill in the identification of new opportunities or requirements to meet challenges in the field and propose changes.
	Language Requirements : Proficient/Fluent level of English is required.


Desirables:


	Knowledge of another official UN language (Arabic, Chinese, French, Russian or Spanish) or a local language is an asset.
	Prior experience working in global health initiatives (Global Fund, GFF, GAVI, Republic of Korea) managing projects in Nigeria or similar country settings or programmes is an asset.
	Preference will be given to candidates who have demonstrated knowledge of and professional experience in at least three of the following areas:
	
		Management of immunization related projects
		Contributions to health systems strengthening funding applications and programmes
		Introduction of innovations in complex contexts
		Experience and knowledge of the Nigerian context
		Experience working in a UN system agency or organization, or in a global health initiative (TGF, GAVI, GFF) or development bank
	
	
	Relevant experience at country level, particularly in development, fragile settings and humanitarian contexts.&nbsp;
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-27",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-04",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "United Nations International Children's Emergency Fund (UNICEF)"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Abuja",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",

  },

    }
  }
}



Project Manager (P-3) Job at United Nations International Children's Emergency Fund (UNICEF)




        
    

        
        United Nations International Children's Emergency Fund (UNICEF) works in some of the world’s toughest places, to reach the world’s most disadvantaged children. To save their lives. To defend their rights. To help them fulfill their potential. Across 190 countries and territories, we work for every child, everywhere, every day, to build a better world for everyone.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager (P-3)

Job no: 594084
Job Location: Abuja
Categories: Programme Management

How can you make a difference?
     

            
    




	As the Project Manager, you will lead overall project planning, scheduling, and execution to ensure the construction and operationalization of 3 vaccine hubs (Abuja and Kano), manage the operation and maintenance contracts for solarization of over 370 health centers, oversee the defect liability period for Abuja Premier Warehouse, operationalize the Lagos Hub and ensure they are all delivered on time, within scope, and within budget, in accordance with approved project objectives and work plans.
	You will report to the Nigeria Country Office Chief of Health and will work with the Health Managers responsible for leading the Primary Health Care, Immunization and maternal, neonatal and child heath (MNCH) clusters.


Major responsibilities


	Support to programme development and planning
	Programme management, monitoring and delivery of results 
	Technical and operational support to programme implementation 
	Networking and partnership building 
	Innovation, knowledge management and capacity building 


To qualify as an advocate for every child you will have
Minimum requirements:


	Education: An advanced University Degree (Master’s or higher) in Health, Public Health Construction Management, Financial Management, Management, International Relations, or closely related field is required.
	Work Experience: A minimum of five years of relevant professional experience in the management of projects within the national/international health sector is required.


Skills:


	Ability to resolve difficult, complex and sensitive situations often under pressure;
	Ability to initiate and manage change in a diverse environment;
	Ability to clearly and concisely express ideas and concepts in written and oral form;
	Ability to allocate appropriate time and resources for successful achievement of goals, and foresee risks and allow for contingencies when planning;
	Ability to implement clear goals that are consistent with agreed strategies, identify priority activities and assignments, adjust priorities as required;
	Ability to work strategically to realize organizational goals, develop strategies, set clear visions;
	Ability to advanced communication and negotiation skills to establish and maintain trusted partnerships for achievement of objectives and to develop close and effective working relationships with diverse stakeholders;
	Ability to skill in the identification of new opportunities or requirements to meet challenges in the field and propose changes.
	Language Requirements : Proficient/Fluent level of English is required.


Desirables:


	Knowledge of another official UN language (Arabic, Chinese, French, Russian or Spanish) or a local language is an asset.
	Prior experience working in global health initiatives (Global Fund, GFF, GAVI, Republic of Korea) managing projects in Nigeria or similar country settings or programmes is an asset.
	Preference will be given to candidates who have demonstrated knowledge of and professional experience in at least three of the following areas:
	
		Management of immunization related projects
		Contributions to health systems strengthening funding applications and programmes
		Introduction of innovations in complex contexts
		Experience and knowledge of the Nigerian context
		Experience working in a UN system agency or organization, or in a global health initiative (TGF, GAVI, GFF) or development bank
	
	
	Relevant experience at country level, particularly in development, fragile settings and humanitarian contexts. 
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "UC Agency, a recruiting agency, is recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Department: Communications and Media
Reports To: Managing Director

Job Summary


	UC Agency is seeking a highly creative, innovative, and results-driven Social Media Officer to manage and grow the company&#39;s digital presence across all social media platforms.
	The Social Media Officer shall be responsible for planning, creating, publishing, monitoring, and evaluating digital content that promotes the company&#39;s products, services, projects, events, and corporate image. The successful candidate will help strengthen brand awareness, increase customer engagement, generate leads, support sales activities, and maintain a positive online reputation for the company
	The ideal candidate must possess excellent communication skills, strong content creation abilities, digital marketing expertise, and a passion for social media excellence.


Key Responsibilities
Social Media Strategy and Planning:


	Develop and implement comprehensive social media strategies aligned with company objectives.
	Create monthly, quarterly, and annual social media plans.
	Establish measurable goals for audience growth, engagement, lead generation, and brand awareness.
	Monitor emerging social media trends and recommend innovative approaches.
	Ensure consistency in branding, messaging, and visual identity across all platforms.


Content Creation and Publishing:


	Create engaging graphics, videos, reels, stories, articles, promotional materials, and advertisements.
	Write compelling captions, posts, blogs, and marketing content.
	Produce original content optimized for Facebook, Instagram, X (Twitter), LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, and other relevant platforms.
	Coordinate photography and videography activities.
	Schedule and publish content using approved social media management tools.
	Ensure timely publication of company announcements, product launches, promotions, and events.


Community Management:


	Monitor all social media accounts daily.
	Respond promptly and professionally to comments, messages, inquiries, and customer complaints.
	Engage followers through polls, discussions, contests, surveys, and interactive campaigns.
	Build and nurture strong online communities around the company
	Escalate sensitive issues to management when necessary.


Digital Marketing Support:


	Support digital advertising campaigns.
	Assist with lead generation activities.
	Promote products, services, training programs, exhibitions, and corporate events.
	Support online sales and customer acquisition initiatives.
	Collaborate with management to execute marketing campaigns successfully.


Event Coverage and Media Support:


	Cover company events, exhibitions, conferences, training sessions, product launches, and corporate activities.
	Produce live social media coverage during events.
	Create event highlight videos and post-event reports.
	Coordinate livestream broadcasts where required


Analytics and Performance Reporting:


	Track key performance indicators across all social media platforms.
	Monitor engagement, impressions, reach, clicks, conversions, and audience growth.
	Prepare weekly and monthly performance reports.
	Analyze data and recommend strategies for continuous improvement.
	Conduct A/B testing where necessary.


Brand and Reputation Management:


	Protect and enhance the online reputation of the company
	Monitor public sentiment and brand perception.
	Ensure all content complies with company policies and standards.
	Support crisis communication and reputation management efforts.


Requirement


	Educational Qualification: Bachelor&#39;s Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Mass Communication, Marketing, Public Relations, Digital Media, Journalism, Communications, Business Administration, Information Technology or any related discipline.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application to: ucjobagency@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Social Media Officer Job at UC Agency




        
    

        
        UC Agency, a recruiting agency, is recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Department: Communications and Media
Reports To: Managing Director

Job Summary
     

            
    




	UC Agency is seeking a highly creative, innovative, and results-driven Social Media Officer to manage and grow the company's digital presence across all social media platforms.
	The Social Media Officer shall be responsible for planning, creating, publishing, monitoring, and evaluating digital content that promotes the company's products, services, projects, events, and corporate image. The successful candidate will help strengthen brand awareness, increase customer engagement, generate leads, support sales activities, and maintain a positive online reputation for the company
	The ideal candidate must possess excellent communication skills, strong content creation abilities, digital marketing expertise, and a passion for social media excellence.


Key Responsibilities
Social Media Strategy and Planning:


	Develop and implement comprehensive social media strategies aligned with company objectives.
	Create monthly, quarterly, and annual social media plans.
	Establish measurable goals for audience growth, engagement, lead generation, and brand awareness.
	Monitor emerging social media trends and recommend innovative approaches.
	Ensure consistency in branding, messaging, and visual identity across all platforms.


Content Creation and Publishing:


	Create engaging graphics, videos, reels, stories, articles, promotional materials, and advertisements.
	Write compelling captions, posts, blogs, and marketing content.
	Produce original content optimized for Facebook, Instagram, X (Twitter), LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, and other relevant platforms.
	Coordinate photography and videography activities.
	Schedule and publish content using approved social media management tools.
	Ensure timely publication of company announcements, product launches, promotions, and events.


Community Management:


	Monitor all social media accounts daily.
	Respond promptly and professionally to comments, messages, inquiries, and customer complaints.
	Engage followers through polls, discussions, contests, surveys, and interactive campaigns.
	Build and nurture strong online communities around the company
	Escalate sensitive issues to management when necessary.


Digital Marketing Support:


	Support digital advertising campaigns.
	Assist with lead generation activities.
	Promote products, services, training programs, exhibitions, and corporate events.
	Support online sales and customer acquisition initiatives.
	Collaborate with management to execute marketing campaigns successfully.


Event Coverage and Media Support:


	Cover company events, exhibitions, conferences, training sessions, product launches, and corporate activities.
	Produce live social media coverage during events.
	Create event highlight videos and post-event reports.
	Coordinate livestream broadcasts where required


Analytics and Performance Reporting:


	Track key performance indicators across all social media platforms.
	Monitor engagement, impressions, reach, clicks, conversions, and audience growth.
	Prepare weekly and monthly performance reports.
	Analyze data and recommend strategies for continuous improvement.
	Conduct A/B testing where necessary.


Brand and Reputation Management:


	Protect and enhance the online reputation of the company
	Monitor public sentiment and brand perception.
	Ensure all content complies with company policies and standards.
	Support crisis communication and reputation management efforts.


Requirement


	Educational Qualification: Bachelor's Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Mass Communication, Marketing, Public Relations, Digital Media, Journalism, Communications, Business Administration, Information Technology or any related discipline.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Software Engineer (Angular)",
  "description": "Africhange (AF) Technology Limited is a remittance company that leverages blockchain technology and P2P exchange to offer a seamless way for users in Canada to send money to Nigeria. Our platform and process are crypto and P2P powered, and our aim is to transform the way people send money to Africa and though currently only serving users in Canada and Nigeria, we are determined to extend operations to all African countries.

Whether you would like to pay for a property, send funds to friends and family or pay employees, you&#39;ll need to safely send funds without stress and ridiculous charges. Africhange is the solution to your problem. We are a safe, secure, and convenient way to send money from Canada to Nigeria; and because we rely on blockchain technology and cryptocurrency, you can be sure that we will always offer you the best way to send money home at the best rates.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Software Engineer (Angular)

Job Location: Lagos (Remote)
Job type: Full-time
Department: Technology

Job Overview


	As a Software Engineer (Angular) at Africhange, you will be a core contributor to the frontend that powers our remittance platform.
	You will design and build responsive, accessible interfaces that customers use to send money across borders &mdash; experiences that must be fast, intuitive, and rock-solid in low-bandwidth environments.&nbsp;
	You&rsquo;ll work closely with backend engineers, product managers, and designers to ship features that directly impact the financial wellbeing of our users.
	This is a high-ownership role.
	You will make meaningful architectural decisions, mentor junior contributors, and help set the standard for frontend engineering quality at Africhange.&nbsp;


Responsibilities
Product Engineering:


	Build and maintain high-performance Angular components for our remittance and KYC flows, exchange rate displays, transaction history, and account management features.
	Implement responsive, mobile-first UI that performs reliably across device types and network conditions common in diaspora markets.
	Integrate with RESTful backend APIs for real-time data such as exchange rates, wallet balances, and payment statuses.
	Develop reusable component libraries and ensure consistent design system adoption across the product.


Quality &amp; Performance:


	Write comprehensive unit and integration tests; maintain high coverage across critical payment flows.
	Profile and optimize Angular application performance - bundle size, lazy loading, change detection, and rendering bottlenecks.
	Conduct code reviews, enforce best practices, and contribute to raising the engineering bar across the frontend team.
	Lead and participate in UAT, functional testing, and regression cycles ahead of releases.


Collaboration &amp; Delivery:


	Collaborate with the design team to translate Figma mockups into pixel-precise, accessible interfaces.
	Work closely with backend engineers to define API contracts, manage state effectively, and ensure smooth end-to-end integration.
	Participate actively in Agile SCRUM ceremonies - sprint planning, stand-ups, retrospectives, and demos.
	Produce clear technical documentation for components, modules, and architectural decisions.
	Coordinate with 3rd-party integration partners (payment processors, identity verification providers) on frontend integration requirements.


Specifications


	Angular (v12+) &mdash; components, services, routing, reactive forms, lazy loading
	TypeScript &mdash; strong typing, interfaces, generics
	HTML5 &amp; CSS3/SCSS &mdash; responsive design, cross-browser compatibility
	RxJS and Observable patterns
	RESTful API integration and HTTP client management
	Git, Git Actions, and CI/CD pipelines
	Unit testing with Jasmine/Karma or Jest
	JavaScript build tools (Webpack, Gulp, or similar)
	Knowledge of Ng Zorro and Ng Bootstrap is an added advantage.


What we offer


	Fully remote opportunity
	Competitive salary
	Access to relevant courses/learning programs
	A great team with a passion for working collaboratively.


Note


	AF offers a great work experience, professional development, challenging careers, and competitive compensation.
	We are an Equal Opportunity Employer.
	Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, or other characteristics.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-28",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

Software Engineer (Angular) Job at Africhange Technology Limited




        
    

        
        Africhange (AF) Technology Limited is a remittance company that leverages blockchain technology and P2P exchange to offer a seamless way for users in Canada to send money to Nigeria. Our platform and process are crypto and P2P powered, and our aim is to transform the way people send money to Africa and though currently only serving users in Canada and Nigeria, we are determined to extend operations to all African countries.
     

            
    



Whether you would like to pay for a property, send funds to friends and family or pay employees, you'll need to safely send funds without stress and ridiculous charges. Africhange is the solution to your problem. We are a safe, secure, and convenient way to send money from Canada to Nigeria; and because we rely on blockchain technology and cryptocurrency, you can be sure that we will always offer you the best way to send money home at the best rates.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Software Engineer (Angular)

Job Location: Lagos (Remote)
Job type: Full-time
Department: Technology

Job Overview


	As a Software Engineer (Angular) at Africhange, you will be a core contributor to the frontend that powers our remittance platform.
	You will design and build responsive, accessible interfaces that customers use to send money across borders — experiences that must be fast, intuitive, and rock-solid in low-bandwidth environments. 
	You’ll work closely with backend engineers, product managers, and designers to ship features that directly impact the financial wellbeing of our users.
	This is a high-ownership role.
	You will make meaningful architectural decisions, mentor junior contributors, and help set the standard for frontend engineering quality at Africhange. 


Responsibilities
Product Engineering:


	Build and maintain high-performance Angular components for our remittance and KYC flows, exchange rate displays, transaction history, and account management features.
	Implement responsive, mobile-first UI that performs reliably across device types and network conditions common in diaspora markets.
	Integrate with RESTful backend APIs for real-time data such as exchange rates, wallet balances, and payment statuses.
	Develop reusable component libraries and ensure consistent design system adoption across the product.


Quality & Performance:


	Write comprehensive unit and integration tests; maintain high coverage across critical payment flows.
	Profile and optimize Angular application performance - bundle size, lazy loading, change detection, and rendering bottlenecks.
	Conduct code reviews, enforce best practices, and contribute to raising the engineering bar across the frontend team.
	Lead and participate in UAT, functional testing, and regression cycles ahead of releases.


Collaboration & Delivery:


	Collaborate with the design team to translate Figma mockups into pixel-precise, accessible interfaces.
	Work closely with backend engineers to define API contracts, manage state effectively, and ensure smooth end-to-end integration.
	Participate actively in Agile SCRUM ceremonies - sprint planning, stand-ups, retrospectives, and demos.
	Produce clear technical documentation for components, modules, and architectural decisions.
	Coordinate with 3rd-party integration partners (payment processors, identity verification providers) on frontend integration requirements.


Specifications


	Angular (v12+) — components, services, routing, reactive forms, lazy loading
	TypeScript — strong typing, interfaces, generics
	HTML5 & CSS3/SCSS — responsive design, cross-browser compatibility
	RxJS and Observable patterns
	RESTful API integration and HTTP client management
	Git, Git Actions, and CI/CD pipelines
	Unit testing with Jasmine/Karma or Jest
	JavaScript build tools (Webpack, Gulp, or similar)
	Knowledge of Ng Zorro and Ng Bootstrap is an added advantage.


What we offer


	Fully remote opportunity
	Competitive salary
	Access to relevant courses/learning programs
	A great team with a passion for working collaboratively.


Note


	AF offers a great work experience, professional development, challenging careers, and competitive compensation.
	We are an Equal Opportunity Employer.
	Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, or other characteristics.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Adviser",
  "description": "Japan Tobacco International (JTI) is a member of the Japan Tobacco Group of Companies. Our 45,000 people come from all over the world, representing more than 100 nationalities. We market world-renowned brands in more than 130 countries with 409 offices, 29 factories, and 8 research &amp; development centers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Adviser

Job Location: Lafia, Nasarawa

About the Role


	As a Business Adviser, you will work closely with business owners, entrepreneurs, and stakeholders to understand their needs and provide strategic guidance.
	Your expertise will enable clients to develop robust business plans, navigate financial challenges, and implement effective marketing strategies.
	The role requires a proactive approach to identifying potential barriers and delivering innovative solutions that align with each client&#39;s goals.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct comprehensive business assessments to identify strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats.
	Advise clients on business planning, financial management, marketing, and operational improvement.
	Support clients in accessing funding opportunities, grants, and other financial resources.
	Develop and deliver workshops, seminars, and resources that enhance client knowledge and skills.
	Maintain up-to-date knowledge of local market conditions, industry trends, and regulatory requirements.
	Build strong, trusting relationships with clients and act as their advocate within the business community.
	Produce regular reports on client progress and program outcomes.


Person Specification


	The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build rapport and inspire confidence in a diverse client base.
	You should be organised, resourceful, and capable of managing multiple projects simultaneously. Previous experience in business development, consultancy, or a related field will be highly advantageous.


Skills and Qualifications:


	A relevant degree or equivalent professional qualification is preferred.
	Proven experience in business advisory, consultancy, or a related area.
	Excellent analytical skills to assess business performance and identify improvement areas.
	Knowledge of financing options and experience supporting clients to secure funding.
	Strong presentation and facilitation skills for delivering training and workshops.
	Competency in using business software and digital tools to aid client development.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

Business Adviser Job at Japan Tobacco International (JTI)




        
    

        
        Japan Tobacco International (JTI) is a member of the Japan Tobacco Group of Companies. Our 45,000 people come from all over the world, representing more than 100 nationalities. We market world-renowned brands in more than 130 countries with 409 offices, 29 factories, and 8 research & development centers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Adviser

Job Location: Lafia, Nasarawa

About the Role
     

            
    




	As a Business Adviser, you will work closely with business owners, entrepreneurs, and stakeholders to understand their needs and provide strategic guidance.
	Your expertise will enable clients to develop robust business plans, navigate financial challenges, and implement effective marketing strategies.
	The role requires a proactive approach to identifying potential barriers and delivering innovative solutions that align with each client's goals.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct comprehensive business assessments to identify strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats.
	Advise clients on business planning, financial management, marketing, and operational improvement.
	Support clients in accessing funding opportunities, grants, and other financial resources.
	Develop and deliver workshops, seminars, and resources that enhance client knowledge and skills.
	Maintain up-to-date knowledge of local market conditions, industry trends, and regulatory requirements.
	Build strong, trusting relationships with clients and act as their advocate within the business community.
	Produce regular reports on client progress and program outcomes.


Person Specification


	The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build rapport and inspire confidence in a diverse client base.
	You should be organised, resourceful, and capable of managing multiple projects simultaneously. Previous experience in business development, consultancy, or a related field will be highly advantageous.


Skills and Qualifications:


	A relevant degree or equivalent professional qualification is preferred.
	Proven experience in business advisory, consultancy, or a related area.
	Excellent analytical skills to assess business performance and identify improvement areas.
	Knowledge of financing options and experience supporting clients to secure funding.
	Strong presentation and facilitation skills for delivering training and workshops.
	Competency in using business software and digital tools to aid client development.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
